item.title: Judge blocks DOJ from releasing Biden's conversations with biographer for 3 weeks
item.description: A judge on Friday cleared the way for the DOJ to disclose former President Joe Biden's conversations with his biographer to the Heritage Foundation. Later in the day, she blocked the release for three weeks.

item.title: James Burrows, co-creator of "Cheers" and prolific director, dies at 85
item.description: James Burrows directed more than 1,000 episodes of television, including every episode of the original "Will & Grace."

item.title: Trump unveils new Air Force One, a $400 million plane gifted by Qatar
item.description: "This is considered the world's most luxurious plane," the president said in front of the enormous new jet.

item.title: What Trump's Reflecting Pool renovation project and the Iran war have in common
item.description: The Reflecting Pool in Washington, D.C., and the war with Iran have nothing to do with one another, but both have similarities worth reflecting upon. CBS News' Major Garrett explains. Then, CBS News' Imtiaz Tyab joins with the latest from Tel Aviv.

item.title: Iran analyst on what could come next for the Middle East
item.description: A ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah appeared to hold on Friday, despite the two sides exchanging strikes overnight. Behnam Ben Taleblu, senior director of FDD's Iran program, joins CBS News to discuss the current situation int he Middle East.

item.title: Questions swirl after 1-year-old boy fatally shot by police in Mississippi
item.description: A police shooting in Senatobia, Mississippi, that left a 1-year-old child dead has ignited simmering tensions between police and Black residents in the small town.

item.title: U.S. clinches spot in World Cup knockout round with dominant win over Australia
item.description: In front of a roaring Seattle crowd, the U.S. men's soccer team​ on Friday defeated Australia in its second World Cup match, clinching a spot in the Round of 32 in the process.

item.title: Company owned by Trump donor won $1.7 million no-bid Reflecting Pool contract
item.description: The federal government awarded a company owned by a Trump donor $1.7 million to install a new water cleaning system for the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool, records show.

item.title: See the full U.S. men's soccer schedule for the 2026 World Cup
item.description: The U.S. men's national soccer team kicked off its 2026 FIFA World Cup campaign against Paraguay at SoFi Stadium on Friday.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: George W. Bush gifts Michelle Obama mints in an ode to their friendship
item.description: The Altoids were a callback to a viral moment between former first lady Michelle Obama and former President George W. Bush.

item.title: 1 dead after trains collide in England, British Transport Police says
item.description: A number of people were also injured after two trains collided north of London on Friday, British Transport Police and a United Kingdom official said.

item.title: Trump unveils Qatari-gifted jet serving as new Air Force One
item.description: President Trump on Friday unveiled the new Air Force One, a $400 million Boeing 747-8 jet, gifted by Qatar.

item.title: Details on delay for next phase of U.S.-Iran talks
item.description: Talks between Iran and the U.S. expected to begin in Switzerland Friday have been postponed. The White House blamed the delay on logistical issues while officials told the Associated Press other outlets that Iran had suspended the talks due to the ongoing fighting in Lebanon. CBS News' Nancy Cordes and Aaron MacLean have more.

item.title: DOJ rebuffs judge's demand to state "anti-weaponization" fund is dead
item.description: A senior Justice Department official called a judge's demand for a declaration on the status of the "anti-weaponization" fund "unnecessary."

item.title: What to know as Israel and Hezbollah reach ceasefire; U.S.-Iran talks delayed
item.description: Israel and Hezbollah reached a ceasefire agreement Friday to end their fighting in Lebanon, three diplomats briefed on the matter told CBS News. Meanwhile, Talks between Iran and the U.S. expected to begin in Switzerland Friday were postponed, with the White House blaming logistical issues for the cancellation of JD Vance's trip to Geneva. CBS News' Robert Berger and Natalie Brand have more.

item.title: A father of 3 wanted to leave wealth to his kids. He turned to a coffee can.
item.description: Matthew Ankrum wanted to build wealth for his daughters and teach them about financial stability. A new book shares how.

item.title: Israel and Hezbollah agree to ceasefire, officials say
item.description: Israel and Hezbollah have reached a Lebanon ceasefire agreement after their fighting delayed progression of the U.S.-Iran deal, a diplomat tells CBS News.

item.title: Expert warns on "bumpy road ahead" for Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire as U.S. seeks Iran deal
item.description: CBS News contributor Aaron MacLean is reacting to the latest Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire news after tensions rose in Lebanon, likely leading to U.S.-Iran talks being postponed.

item.title: Here's who qualifies for the Trump adminstration's student loan rate cut
item.description: The temporary discount applies to eligible federal Direct Loan borrowers who use automatic payments.

item.title: Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire now in effect after tensions flare up
item.description: An Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire is now in effect after tensions rose and impacted the U.S.-Iran talks. CBS News' Imtiaz Tyab and Natalie Brand report.

item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (June 21)
item.description: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Jane Pauley.

item.title: How the Iran war united, and then divided Trump and Netanyahu
item.description: Trump has appeared during the Iran war to lose patience with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, who may now find himself "stuck."

item.title: Italy nixes envoy's visit as leader Meloni "stunned" by Trump's remarks
item.description: Italian outlet says after a meeting at the G7 summit, Trump said Meloni had "begged me to take a picture with her," and he "felt sorry for her."

item.title: What's missing from the Epstein files? Here's what we found
item.description: The Justice Department says it's released "every document required by the Epstein Files Transparency Act," but CBS News has identified numerous gaps.

item.title: Scientists discover parasite that preys on insect-killing "zombie fungus"
item.description: Scientists say the "hyperparasite" preys on "zombie fungus" known to infect insects before subjecting them to a gruesome death.

item.title: Cuba approves free-market reforms in effort to stave off economic collapse
item.description: Cuban lawmakers Thursday adopted nearly 200 historic free-market reforms aimed at rescuing the communist island from a severe crisis aggravated by a U.S. oil blockade.

item.title: Researchers find "Pink Planet" has salty clouds: "We were very surprised"
item.description: The "Pink Planet," formally known as GJ504b, was discovered in 2013 and is technically not a planet but rather a "planetary-mass companion."

item.title: Rips appear in Reflecting Pool's new sealant after algae turn water green
item.description: President Trump's efforts to spruce up the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool faced a snag this week, with algae turning the water green while rips appeared in an "American Flag Blue" surface picked by the president.

item.title: Gas prices fall below $4 a gallon for the first time in nearly 3 months
item.description: The national average for a gallon of regular gas in the U.S. drops to $3.99, the lowest since March 30.
